attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who commits to all-day escapes rather than late attacks. Prefers technical finales where positioning and sprinting from small groups decide the outcome.
team_tactics: Benefits from team morale and support (attacked day after teammate's win), but appears to operate independently in breakaways with limited dedicated domestique support in finale situations.
terrain_preference: Technical finales with corners in final kilometers that favor positioning over pure power. Comfortable in long breakaways on rolling terrain but not a pure climber or sprinter.
vulnerability: Exposed when forced into leadout or tactical racing roles against savvier opponents. Conservative decision-making when race-winning moves occur (failed to counter Skelmose). Lacks experience in stage-winning tactics.
key_patterns: Commits to all-day breakaways rather than late-race attacks (Tour stage 13, 14)
Shows reluctance in tactical leadout situations, exploited by more experienced riders (Tour x2)
Conservative racing when in podium position, prioritizes safe result over attacking for win
Strong technical bike handling through corner sequences, uses for sprint positioning advantage
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 podium showed consistent ability to reach selections but revealed tactical conservatism compared to aggressive 2022 Tour breakthrough
